_meta.description: Curated SVG logos for verticals the open sources (LobeHub / SVGL / Iconify / Devicon / Simple Icons) miss. Trademark posture: nominative fair use — used to identify the brand referenced in a workbook, not modified, not used as endorsement. Each entry must cite source URL + license. See ../data/logos-pack/README.md for the contribution process.

fda.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 200 80"><rect width="200" height="80" fill="#003e7e"/><text x="100" y="52" font-family="Arial,sans-serif" font-size="42" font-weight="700" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle">FDA</text></svg>
fda.source: hand-set wordmark (US Food and Drug Administration)
fda.license: US government work — public domain (17 USC § 105)
fda.notes: Simple wordmark. Replace with official seal when needed.

ema.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 200 80"><rect width="200" height="80" fill="#003399"/><text x="100" y="52" font-family="Arial,sans-serif" font-size="38" font-weight="700" fill="#ffcc00" text-anchor="middle">EMA</text></svg>
ema.license: EU institutional — public domain for non-commercial use; contact agency for commercial
ema.notes: Simple wordmark in EU flag colors.

nasa.source: hand-set wordmark (NASA — original meatball is at https://www.nasa.gov/wp-content/themes/nasa/assets/images/nasa-logo.svg)
nasa.license: US government work — public domain (17 USC § 105). Note: NASA logos have specific usage rules; check brand.nasa.gov
nasa.notes: Replace with official meatball SVG when needed.
